source_quote: In the primary intention-to-treat analysis, the rate of hospitalization was not statistically different in patients treated with HD and with PD (adjusted rate ratio: 1.21; 95% confidence limits: 0.55, 2.66)
adjusted rate ratio: 1.21; 95% confidence limits: 0.55, 2.66 ... Total hospitalizations: PD 74, HD 153; PD 0 hospitalizations: 63 (59%), HD 0 hospitalizations: 121 (58%)

outcome: Physician-evaluated change in severity of symptoms and signs (short-term)
source_quote: QMG score: IVIg 6.1 (3.8) vs Placebo 6.6 (3.5), p = 0.653. Changes in the QMG score in the two study groups were similar. Intergroup differences in the QMG score over the 14 days of the study compared with baseline were not statistically significant.
Length of hospital stay, days: All patients 3.7 (3.7); IVIg 3.2 (2.7); Placebo 4.2 (4.5); p = 0.586
SAEs of MG exacerbations or MG crises were reported in 7 patients: 4 patients (13.3%) in the IGIV-C treatment group and 3 in the placebo group (10.0%).

The most common TEAEs (>15%) in the IGIV-C treatment group were headache, MG worsening, upper respiratory tract infection, and nausea. Exact event counts for headache per arm not reported in the paper.
There was 1 death in the IGIV-C group and 2 deaths in the placebo group.
